title: Ascendium Senior Program Officer Jobs 2026: Multiple High-Impact Philanthropy Positions Offering Approximately $125,000 Salary
summary: Professionals passionate about expanding educational access, improving workforce training outcomes, and creating pathways to economic mobility now have an exciting opportunity to join Ascendium, a leading philanthropic organization committed to helping learners from low-income backgrounds succeed in postsecondary education and workforce training. Ascendium is currently recruiting for multiple Senior Program Officer positions to help lead and execute strategic grantmaking portfolios focused on expanding opportunity, supporting learner success, and connecting and aligning systems across the education and workforce sectors. These positions offer experienced professionals the chance to influence national education and workforce development initiatives, collaborate with leading organizations, fund innovative solutions, and generate knowledge that improves outcomes for underserved learners across the United States. Job Overview The Senior Program Officer serves as a lead strategy execution officer within one of Ascendium’s core philanthropic strategy teams: Expand Opportunity Support Learner Success Connect and Align Systems Reporting directly to a Strategy Director, successful candidates will play a major role in designing, implementing, managing, and evaluating strategic grantmaking portfolios that align with Ascendium’s mission and philanthropic goals. The role combines strategic leadership, grantmaking expertise, partnership development, policy engagement, learning and impact evaluation, and field-building activities. Salary and Compensation Ascendium plans to offer an annual salary of approximately: $125,000 per year Candidates with significant experience and qualifications may be eligible for higher compensation. In addition to a competitive salary, Ascendium offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support employee wellbeing, financial security, and professional growth. Location and Work Arrangement The position is primarily based in: Madison, Wisconsin, United States Ascendium operates a hybrid work model that includes: Three days per week working in the office. Two days per week working remotely. Candidates are expected to reside in or relocate to Madison, Wisconsin. However, Ascendium may consider remote candidates if qualified Madison-based applicants cannot be identified. Travel Requirements Successful candidates should be prepared to travel approximately: 6–10 times per year Travel may include: National conferences Strategic convenings Grant partner events Site visits Home weeks Other mission-aligned activities Areas of Expertise Being Sought Ascendium is hiring multiple Senior Program Officers and applicants should possess expertise in at least one of the following areas. 1. Workforce Reentry Ecosystems Experience involving: Workforce training Reentry supports Justice-impacted populations Local workforce systems Regional workforce partnerships National workforce organizations 2. Community College Transformation Expertise related to: Guided pathways Developmental education reform Institutional transformation Student success redesign Community college system improvement Postsecondary completion strategies 3. State-Level Cross-Sector Systems Alignment Experience involving: Postsecondary education systems Workforce development systems Corrections systems State policy implementation Data infrastructure development Dual enrollment programs Credit mobility initiatives 4. External Affairs Knowledge and experience in: State policy analysis Federal policy analysis Coalition building Strategic communications Public affairs Stakeholder engagement Field leadership Applicants are encouraged to clearly identify their area or areas of expertise in their cover letter. Key Responsibilities Senior Program Officers will have a broad range of responsibilities aimed at advancing Ascendium’s strategic objectives. Strategic Portfolio Leadership Responsibilities include: Designing strategic grantmaking portfolios. Managing grantmaking initiatives. Reviewing portfolio performance. Aligning grant investments with organizational goals. Developing responsive grantmaking priorities. Grantmaking and Funding Management Successful candidates will: Source ideas and potential partners. Assess strategic alignment of proposals. Guide applicants through the grant process. Develop funding recommendations. Draft funding proposals for board review. Structure reporting and payment processes. Learning and Impact Development Working closely with Senior Learning and Impact Officers, Senior Program Officers will: Develop learning agendas. Monitor strategic outcomes. Evaluate grant effectiveness. Identify lessons from funded initiatives. Improve future grantmaking strategies. Partner Support and Relationship Building The role includes: Supporting grant partners. Facilitating connections among organizations. Building collaborative networks. Strengthening partnerships with peer funders. Promoting knowledge sharing across the field. Convenings and Field Leadership Senior Program Officers will also: Lead strategic events and convenings. Facilitate discussions among stakeholders. Present at conferences. Participate in national working groups. Engage with professional associations. Contribute thought leadership pieces and publications. Team Collaboration and Mentorship Additional responsibilities include: Collaborating with colleagues across strategy teams. Mentoring Program Officers and Associate Program Officers. Supporting organizational planning and budgeting. Coordinating strategic communications efforts. Required Qualifications Candidates should possess a strong combination of education, leadership experience, and subject matter expertise. Educational Requirements Applicants should have knowledge equivalent to a master’s degree in fields such as: Education Philanthropy Social Sciences Social Work Community Development Economics Business Related disciplines Professional Experience Candidates should have: At least seven years of relevant experience. Experience in postsecondary education. Workforce development or workforce training experience. Philanthropic sector experience. Nonprofit leadership experience. Local, state, or federal government experience. Essential Skills Applicants should demonstrate: Commitment to equitable opportunity. Strong project management skills. Strategic thinking abilities. Stakeholder engagement expertise. Relationship-building skills. Leadership experience. Excellent written communication skills. Excellent verbal communication skills. Adaptability and problem-solving abilities. Ability to work collaboratively across sectors. Benefits Package Ascendium offers a highly competitive employee benefits package that includes: Health Coverage Medical insurance Dental insurance Vision insurance Family Support Up to eight weeks of paid parental leave Education Benefits Student loan repayment assistance Tuition reimbursement opportunities Retirement Planning Retirement savings plan Employer matching contributions These benefits are designed to help employees achieve long-term personal and professional success. Equal Opportunity Employment Ascendium is committed to equal employment opportunity and welcomes applications from qualified individuals regardless of: Race Color Religion Sex Age National origin Disability status Veteran status Other protected characteristics The organization actively promotes diversity, inclusion, and equitable opportunity in both its workplace and philanthropic initiatives. Important Immigration Information Applicants should note that: H-1B sponsorship, including initiation or transfer, is not available for this position. Candidates should ensure they have appropriate authorization to work in the United States before applying. Click here for more and to apply Why This Opportunity Stands Out The Senior Program Officer role at Ascendium offers far more than a traditional nonprofit or philanthropic position. It provides an opportunity to influence national systems, improve educational outcomes, shape workforce development initiatives, and create meaningful change for learners from low-income backgrounds. Professionals with expertise in education, workforce development, policy, philanthropy, community college reform, or systems transformation will find this role especially attractive due to its strategic impact, leadership opportunities, competitive compensation, and mission-driven focus. For experienced professionals looking to drive large-scale change in education and workforce systems, this opportunity represents a unique chance to make a lasting impact while advancing a rewarding career in philanthropy. click here for more global job opportunities The post Ascendium Senior Program Officer Jobs 2026: Multiple High-Impact Philanthropy Positions Offering Approximately $125,000 Salary appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Deputy Director, People Operations at YLabs (Remote): Lead Global HR Strategy, Pay Equity, Compliance, and Organizational Growth
summary: YLabs, a globally recognized design and research organization dedicated to empowering young people through innovative technologies and youth-centered solutions, is currently recruiting a Deputy Director, People Operations . This exciting leadership opportunity offers experienced human resources and people operations professionals the chance to shape the future of a rapidly growing international nonprofit organization. The role is fully remote, open to candidates worldwide, with a preference for professionals based in Africa. The successful candidate will play a critical role in developing and overseeing YLabs global people strategy, compensation systems, compliance infrastructure, talent acquisition frameworks, and organizational culture as the organization expands across Africa and Asia. About YLabs Founded in 2014 at the Harvard Innovation Lab, YLabs has become a leading global organization specializing in the design and implementation of technologies that amplify youth agency, opportunity, and wellbeing. YLabs collaborates directly with young people to co-create innovative solutions that address some of the world s most pressing challenges. Through its unique youth-centered design methodology, the organization ensures that young people actively shape the programs and technologies intended to improve their lives. The organization operates globally with headquarters in Rwanda and the United States and has successfully implemented projects across 18 countries. YLabs focuses on three major impact areas: Optimum Health and Wellness Improved Economic Opportunity Increased Climate Resilience Their multidisciplinary team includes experts in: Human-centered design Adolescent health Climate resilience Livelihood development Behavioral science Digital product innovation Epidemiology Research and evaluation Position Overview The Deputy Director, People Operations will serve as a senior leader responsible for building and strengthening YLabs’ global human resources and operations systems during a critical period of organizational growth. This is not merely an HR management role. The successful candidate will function as a department head and strategic advisor to the Senior Leadership Team, helping to ensure that YLabs remains equitable, compliant, efficient, and people-centered as it scales internationally. The position involves designing systems that support a globally distributed workforce while promoting employee wellbeing, organizational effectiveness, and legal compliance across multiple countries. Job Details Position Title Deputy Director, People Operations Department Operations Finance Employment Type Full-Time Work Arrangement Remote Contract Duration Initial 12-month contract Experience Level Executive / Senior Leadership Salary $50,000–$61,818 USD annually (gross) Compensation is based on experience and regional benchmarks and is paid in the local currency of the employee’s permanent work location. Why This Opportunity Stands Out This position offers a rare chance to influence the strategic direction of a globally respected nonprofit organization while leading initiatives that impact employees across multiple continents. Key highlights include: Fully remote work arrangement Competitive international salary Opportunity to lead global HR transformation Strategic leadership role Influence organizational growth and expansion Work with multicultural teams across continents Drive global pay equity initiatives Contribute to youth empowerment worldwide Comprehensive employee benefits package Key Responsibilities 1. Global People Strategy The selected candidate will: Develop and implement a global pay equity system Design salary bands using regional and national benchmarks Strengthen performance management systems Improve employee feedback processes Modernize organizational leveling frameworks Align people systems with organizational growth goals 2. Talent Acquisition Leadership Responsibilities include: Creating international recruitment strategies Developing hiring policies for employees and contractors Forecasting talent needs across departments Building YLabs employer brand Attracting top global talent Supporting workforce planning initiatives 3. Global Policy Development The Deputy Director will: Develop and implement organizational policies Review existing operational frameworks Address policy gaps affecting remote teams Ensure policies reflect organizational values Promote equity and inclusion through policy design Support sustainable organizational scaling 4. Legal and Global Compliance The role requires: Designing global compliance strategies Advising leadership on country-specific employment models Managing compliance across multiple jurisdictions Supporting legal employment structures Ensuring adherence to labor regulations Overseeing compliance within YLabs U.S. and Rwanda entities 5. Team Leadership and Development Responsibilities include: Supervising HR and Operations staff Coaching and mentoring team members Building organizational people-operations capacity Strengthening leadership capabilities Supporting employee growth and development 6. Human Resources Operations The successful candidate will oversee: Payroll administration Employee benefits programs HR information systems (HRIS) Personnel management processes Organizational workflows Employee relations matters 7. Learning and Employee Experience Additional responsibilities include: Designing learning and development strategies Establishing employee progression pathways Succession planning for key positions Supporting wellness initiatives Managing employee engagement programs Leading annual staff feedback initiatives Ideal Candidate Profile YLabs seeks an individual who combines strategic thinking with empathy and operational excellence. The ideal candidate is: A systems builder who creates sustainable organizational structures A strong people leader who fosters trust and wellbeing Skilled at managing difficult conversations Culturally competent and inclusive Comfortable making high-level strategic decisions Data-driven in decision-making Passionate about equity and organizational effectiveness Committed to YLabs’ mission and values Required Qualifications Applicants should possess: Professional Experience Minimum of 10 years of progressive HR, talent management, or people operations experience Significant leadership-level experience Experience in international nonprofit organizations (INGOs) Experience supporting organizations with substantial operations in Africa Experience leading global people operations initiatives Technical Expertise Compensation design and salary framework development Multi-country employment compliance International workforce management HR systems administration Organizational policy development Talent acquisition and workforce planning Leadership Skills Team supervision and development Cross-cultural management experience Strategic planning capabilities Organizational transformation expertise Language Requirement Fluency in written and spoken English Preferred Qualifications Additional advantages include: Experience supporting organizational growth or transformation Knowledge of Project Fair pay equity frameworks Degree in: Human Resources Business Administration Law Related disciplines Professional HR certifications such as: CIPD SHRM CHRP YLabs Commitment to Pay Equity YLabs follows the principles established by Project Fair, promoting the concept of equal pay for work of equal value as outlined in the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Salary structures are carefully designed to: Ensure fairness Promote transparency Reduce pay disparities Reflect local market conditions Support equitable employment practices globally Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion YLabs is deeply committed to creating an inclusive workplace where employees from diverse backgrounds can thrive. The organization strongly encourages applications from individuals of all: Cultural backgrounds Ethnicities Educational experiences Socioeconomic backgrounds Gender identities Sexual orientations Age groups Physical abilities YLabs maintains a strict non-discrimination policy throughout its recruitment and employment practices. Application Process Interested candidates should submit their applications through the YLabs online careers portal. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. Deadline No specific application deadline has been announced. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. Early applications are strongly encouraged. Final Thoughts The Deputy Director, People Operations position at YLabs presents an exceptional opportunity for experienced HR and people operations leaders to influence the growth of a globally respected organization dedicated to youth empowerment. With competitive compensation, a fully remote work arrangement, and the chance to shape global people systems across multiple continents, this role is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact at the intersection of leadership, equity, compliance, and organizational development. title: IIE Scholar Rescue Fund (IIE-SRF) Fellowship 2026: Complete Application Guide for Threatened and Displaced Scholars Worldwide
summary: The IIE Scholar Rescue Fund (IIE-SRF) Fellowship 2026 offers a critical lifeline for scholars, researchers, and academics who are facing threats, persecution, conflict, violence, or other circumstances that prevent them from safely continuing their academic work in their home countries. Administered by the Institute of International Education (IIE), the Scholar Rescue Fund provides fellowship support to qualified scholars whose academic careers and personal safety are at risk. Through this prestigious fellowship, scholars can continue their research, teaching, publishing, and other academic activities at host institutions in safe locations around the world. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year, with fellowships awarded quarterly. Application Deadline For consideration at the September 2026 Selection Committee meeting, applications must be submitted no later than July 26, 2026 . Although the program accepts applications on a rolling basis, applicants are encouraged to submit complete applications as early as possible. What Is the IIE Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship? The IIE Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship is designed to support scholars who are unable to continue their academic work due to threats, violence, political instability, armed conflict, persecution, or other dangerous circumstances in their home countries. The fellowship enables qualified academics to: Continue their research activities. Pursue teaching opportunities. Publish scholarly work. Maintain academic productivity. Preserve academic freedom. Continue contributing to global knowledge and scholarship. Work safely at host institutions outside their home country. The program recognizes the essential role scholars play in advancing education, innovation, research, and intellectual development worldwide. Why the IIE-SRF Fellowship Matters Across many regions of the world, scholars face significant challenges that threaten both their professional careers and personal safety. These challenges may include: Armed conflict and war. Political persecution. Restrictions on academic freedom. Targeted threats against researchers and educators. Harassment based on academic work. Institutional collapse due to crises. Human rights violations affecting academics. Security risks that make scholarly work impossible. The IIE-SRF Fellowship helps preserve academic careers while ensuring that scholars can continue contributing valuable research and expertise to the global academic community. Fellowship Application Process Scholars can submit their applications directly to the IIE Scholar Rescue Fund by email. In situations where an applicant cannot personally submit the materials, a third party may submit the application on the scholar’s behalf. Required Application Materials Applicants must prepare and submit several important documents to support their fellowship application. 1. Completed Application Form Every applicant must complete and submit the official IIE-SRF application form. 2. Current Curriculum Vitae (CV) or Resume The CV or resume should be detailed and include comprehensive information about the applicant’s academic and professional background. The CV must include: Academic Degrees Provide details for all earned degrees, including: Degree-granting institution. Academic field or discipline. Date the degree was awarded. Current Academic Position Include: Institution location. Employment dates. Professional title. Primary responsibilities and duties. Previous Academic Positions List all previous academic appointments in chronological order, including: Institution name. Location. Employment dates. Professional title. Key responsibilities. Academic Publications Applicants should provide a detailed publication record, including: Journal articles. Books. Book chapters. Research reports. Exhibitions. Other scholarly outputs. Each publication should include: Full citation. Authors. Publication title. Publication date. Page numbers where applicable. Additional Academic Contributions Applicants should also include: Master s thesis supervision. Ph.D. supervision. Conference presentations. Research grants. Honors and awards. Professional affiliations. Other significant academic achievements. 3. Academic Statement Applicants must submit a thorough academic statement of at least one page. The statement should explain: Academic background. Professional experience. Research interests. Areas of specialization. Academic accomplishments. Teaching experience. Research activities. Publishing activities. Proposed fellowship activities. Applicants should clearly describe what they intend to accomplish during the fellowship period, including future research, teaching, publishing, or related scholarly activities. 4. Personal Statement A personal statement of at least one page is required. The statement should provide: Reasons for applying to IIE-SRF. Detailed explanation of threats faced. Description of risks and challenges. Circumstances preventing continued work in the home country. Need for fellowship support. Impact of current conditions on academic work and personal safety. Applicants are encouraged to provide clear, detailed, and factual information that helps reviewers understand their circumstances. Reference Letter Requirements IIE-SRF requires a minimum of three original reference letters. Additional letters may be requested when necessary. Letters from colleagues both within and outside the applicant’s home country are preferred. Academic and Professional Reference Letters Applicants should provide academic or professional references from colleagues who can evaluate: Research quality. Publications. Teaching effectiveness. Academic achievements. Professional contributions. Specialized expertise. These references should demonstrate the applicant’s scholarly qualifications and impact within their field. Personal Reference Letters Applicants should also submit personal references from individuals familiar with their situation. These references should explain: Threats faced by the applicant. Personal circumstances. Security concerns. Reasons the applicant requires protection and support. Important Reference Letter Guidelines Reference letters should: Be original and individualized. Reflect the referee’s direct knowledge of the applicant. Avoid identical wording. Avoid AI-generated content. Be prepared on official letterhead whenever possible. Be sent directly to IIE-SRF when feasible. Additional Supporting Documents Applicants must also provide: Copy of Highest Degree Submit a copy of the Ph.D. degree or the highest academic degree earned. Recent Research Publications Provide samples of the most recent scholarly publications. Host Institution Letter (Optional) Applicants who have already secured a host institution may submit a letter confirming the institution’s intention to host the fellowship if awarded. This document is optional and not required for application consideration. Minimum Documents Needed for Initial Review To begin evaluating an application, IIE-SRF must receive: Application Form. Current CV. Academic Statement. Personal Statement. The remaining supporting documents may be submitted later. Important Policy on Originality and AI-Generated Content IIE-SRF places significant importance on authenticity and originality. All application materials must: Be unique to the individual applicant. Reflect personal academic experiences. Reflect personal circumstances. Avoid copied content. Properly cite borrowed information. The program strongly discourages the use of artificial intelligence tools, including ChatGPT, for preparing application materials or answering program-related questions. According to IIE-SRF guidelines, suspected AI-generated language may negatively affect an application during the assessment process. Applicants should therefore ensure that all submitted materials genuinely reflect their own experiences, academic achievements, and personal circumstances. Special Guidance for Married Applicants Spouses who both qualify for the fellowship and intend to apply should submit their applications simultaneously. Applicants should note: Each spouse must submit a separate application. Each application must contain unique materials. Shared or duplicated application content is not permitted. Delayed submission by one spouse after the other has already received a fellowship award may affect consideration. How Applications Are Reviewed IIE-SRF conducts a comprehensive assessment of every application. Reviewers evaluate: Academic qualifications. Research achievements. Scholarly contributions. Publication record. Professional accomplishments. Nature of the threats faced. Urgency of the applicant’s situation. Potential fellowship impact. During the review process, IIE-SRF may: Contact references. Consult independent experts. Seek information about the applicant’s academic field. Request clarification from applicants. Verify details regarding reported threats and circumstances. The organization states that identifying information will not be shared without the applicant’s permission. Selection and Fellowship Awards Fellowships are awarded quarterly through a formal selection process. Because demand for support is extremely high, meeting eligibility requirements does not automatically guarantee fellowship funding. Applicants are encouraged to submit complete, compelling, and well-documented applications that clearly demonstrate both scholarly excellence and urgent need. Click here for more and to apply Final Thoughts The IIE Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship 2026 represents a unique opportunity for threatened scholars to continue their academic careers in safety while preserving their contributions to global research, teaching, and intellectual advancement. Scholars facing serious risks in their home countries are encouraged to prepare strong, original applications and submit them before the July 26, 2026 deadline for consideration in the September 2026 review cycle. Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post IIE Scholar Rescue Fund (IIE-SRF) Fellowship 2026: Complete Application Guide for Threatened and Displaced Scholars Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship Applications Open: A Unique Opportunity for UK Civil Servants to Collaborate with Leading Academics and Shape Evidence-Based Policy
summary: The Office for Institutional and Thematic Studies (OITS), through its Policy Insights Unit (PIU), has officially opened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Policy Fellowship Programme . This prestigious fellowship offers UK-based civil servants and public officials an opportunity to deepen their policy expertise, work closely with leading academics, and develop research that can contribute to better policymaking across government. The fellowship is designed to strengthen the relationship between academia and government by enabling policymakers to engage directly with cutting-edge research while developing practical solutions to real-world policy challenges. Application Deadline: 15 July 2026 What is the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship? The OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship is a six-month programme that brings together civil servants and academic experts from leading UK universities. During the fellowship period, participants will undertake policy-focused research under the guidance of an academic mentor and produce a research output relevant to their area of policy responsibility. The programme aims to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and public policy implementation, ensuring that government decisions are informed by high-quality evidence and innovative thinking. This initiative represents the fourth edition of the fellowship programme and builds upon the success of previous cohorts. In 2025, twenty civil servants from twelve government departments participated in the programme, collaborating with academics to produce impactful policy research and recommendations. Fellowship Partner Universities Successful fellows will receive mentorship from academics affiliated with some of the United Kingdom’s respected higher education institutions, including: Brunel University London Lancaster University University of Surrey University of York These institutions provide expertise across a broad range of disciplines and policy areas, ensuring fellows receive relevant guidance tailored to their research interests. Research Areas Available The 2026 fellowship offers participants the opportunity to explore an expanded range of policy themes and research areas. Areas of interest include, but are not limited to: 1. Health and Wellbeing Participants may investigate public health challenges, healthcare delivery systems, mental health policies, community wellbeing initiatives, and strategies for improving health outcomes. 2. Sustainability and Net Zero Research may focus on climate policy, environmental sustainability, green transitions, renewable energy, and pathways toward achieving net-zero targets. 3. Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technologies Fellows can explore how technological advancements are shaping society, governance, regulation, innovation, and economic development. 4. Governance and Public Administration Research opportunities exist around public sector reform, institutional effectiveness, policymaking processes, accountability, and public service delivery. 5. Global Systems and International Relationships Participants may investigate international cooperation, geopolitical developments, trade relationships, and global governance challenges. 6. Education and Social Justice Potential topics include educational equity, social mobility, inclusion, workforce development, and policies aimed at reducing inequalities. 7. Creative and Cultural Futures Research may examine the role of arts, culture, creative industries, and cultural policy in economic and social development. 8. Economic Development Participants can focus on economic growth, productivity, innovation ecosystems, labour markets, entrepreneurship, and regional development. Benefits of the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship The fellowship provides numerous professional and personal development opportunities for participants. Academic Mentorship Fellows will receive direct support from leading academics with specialist expertise relevant to their policy area. Enhanced Research Skills Participants will strengthen their abilities in: Data analysis Evidence interpretation Policy evaluation Research design Knowledge synthesis Expanded Professional Networks The programme creates valuable connections between: Civil servants Government departments Academic institutions Policy researchers Subject matter experts Increased Subject Expertise Participants gain deeper knowledge of their policy area and access to the latest academic research and evidence. Improved Policy Impact By integrating academic insight into policymaking, fellows are better positioned to develop innovative and evidence-based solutions to public challenges. Who Should Apply? The programme is seeking applications from UK-based civil servants and public officials who wish to strengthen their policy knowledge and engage more deeply with research. Applicants should have a strong interest in evidence-informed policymaking and be prepared to dedicate time to conducting policy-related research during the fellowship period. The fellowship is particularly suitable for individuals looking to: Improve policy development skills Expand professional networks Strengthen analytical capabilities Collaborate with academic experts Produce impactful policy research Application Process Applications for the OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship are now open, and the application form is available online. Interested candidates should: Review the official Fellowship Q A document . Carefully examine eligibility requirements. Complete the official application form. Submit all required materials before the deadline. Applicants with questions regarding the fellowship can contact the programme team through the official fellowship communication channels provided by the Policy Insights Unit. Important Deadline Application Deadline: Wednesday, 15 July 2026 Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their applications early to ensure sufficient time for reviewing eligibility criteria, developing research ideas, and completing all application requirements. Why This Fellowship Matters Modern governments face increasingly complex challenges that require evidence-based and interdisciplinary solutions. The OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship provides a valuable platform for policymakers to engage directly with academic expertise and generate research-driven insights that can improve public policy outcomes. By fostering collaboration between government and academia, the fellowship contributes to more informed decision-making, stronger policy development, and better public services. For civil servants seeking to expand their expertise and make a meaningful impact within government, this programme represents an exceptional professional development opportunity. Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here The post OITS 2026 Policy Fellowship Applications Open: A Unique Opportunity for UK Civil Servants to Collaborate with Leading Academics and Shape Evidence-Based Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027 Applications Open: Earn a $500 Scholarship, Develop Leadership Skills, and Become a Youth Advocate Against Child Sexual Exploitation
summary: Applications are now open for the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship 2026–2027 , a transformative virtual youth leadership program designed to empower young people with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities needed to become effective advocates, advisors, and changemakers within their communities. Organized by OneChild, the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) is a free virtual scholarship and empowerment program that equips young people with leadership skills while engaging them in the global effort to end the sexual exploitation of children. The fellowship provides a unique opportunity for youth to gain practical advocacy experience, receive professional mentorship, and contribute to meaningful social change. Young people who are passionate about community development, child protection, leadership, and advocacy are encouraged to apply before the application deadline. Application Deadline: August 15, 2026 What is the Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) Leadership Accelerator Fellowship? The Youth Advisory Squad (YAS) is a youth-centered leadership development initiative that seeks to engage and empower young leaders to become ambassadors, advisors, and advocates within their communities and beyond. The fellowship aims to provide participants with the tools and knowledge necessary to understand and address issues related to the sexual exploitation of children while developing leadership and professional skills that can support their future educational and career goals. Through a structured virtual learning experience, fellows actively participate in discussions, training sessions, advocacy activities, and community projects designed to create real-world impact. Program Duration and Structure The YAS Leadership Accelerator Fellowship runs from September 2026 to June 2027 . The program is entirely virtual, making it accessible to participants regardless of their location. Key program features include: Virtual participation from home Biweekly meetings held every other Saturday Interactive leadership development sessions Expert-led training workshops Community engagement opportunities Collaborative learning with fellow youth leaders This flexible online format allows participants to balance their educational commitments while actively engaging in the fellowship experience. What Participants Will Learn The fellowship provides extensive training in leadership and advocacy-related areas that are essential for young changemakers. Participants will receive training in: 1. Advocacy Skills Fellows will learn how to advocate effectively for issues affecting children and youth while influencing positive change within their communities. 2. Public Speaking Participants will develop confidence and communication skills necessary for speaking in public settings, presentations, meetings, and advocacy campaigns. 3. Facilitation Skills The program teaches youth how to lead discussions, coordinate group activities, and facilitate meaningful conversations within community and organizational settings. 4. Action Planning Fellows will learn how to design and implement practical projects that address social issues and create measurable impact. 5. Child Protection Awareness Participants will gain a deeper understanding of: Sexual exploitation of children Child protection challenges Prevention strategies Community awareness approaches Youth participation in safeguarding efforts Capstone Community Project One of the most important components of the fellowship is the mandatory capstone project. Each participant will be required to design and implement a project within their school or community that addresses issues connected to child protection, youth advocacy, awareness creation, or social impact. The capstone project allows fellows to apply the knowledge and leadership skills gained throughout the fellowship while making a direct contribution to their communities. Learn from Industry Experts and Guest Speakers Throughout the fellowship, participants will engage with guest speakers and professionals from various sectors. These experts provide valuable insights into: Leadership development Youth engagement Advocacy campaigns Social impact initiatives Community organizing Career development pathways Exposure to experienced professionals helps participants expand their networks and gain practical knowledge that can support their future aspirations. Opportunity to Shape Youth Engagement Strategies An important aspect of the fellowship is the opportunity for participants to contribute directly to OneChild s youth engagement initiatives. Fellows will: Share perspectives and experiences Provide feedback on youth programs Offer recommendations for future initiatives Help strengthen youth-focused strategies This advisory role ensures that young voices remain central to decision-making processes affecting youth programs. Benefits of the YAS Leadership Accelerator Fellowship Successful completion of the fellowship comes with several valuable benefits. Financial Support Each fellow who successfully completes the program receives: A $500 Scholarship This scholarship serves as recognition of the fellow s commitment, participation, and contribution throughout the program. Professional Recognition Graduates receive: A personalized letter of reference A certificate of completion These credentials can strengthen future scholarship, internship, educational, and employment applications. Internship Opportunities Top-performing fellows and alumni gain access to: Competitive and limited paid Summer Internship opportunities with OneChild. These internships provide additional professional experience and career development opportunities. Leadership and Career Development Participants also benefit from: Enhanced leadership skills Increased confidence Expanded professional networks Community engagement experience Career readiness development Real-world advocacy experience Why You Should Apply The Youth Advisory Squad Leadership Accelerator Fellowship is more than a leadership program. It is an opportunity for young people to become active participants in addressing important social challenges while developing skills that will benefit them throughout their academic and professional journeys. By joining the fellowship, participants gain valuable leadership experience, build meaningful connections, contribute to child protection efforts, and position themselves for future educational and career opportunities. Application Deadline Applications are currently open and will close on: August 15, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible because interviews will be scheduled immediately after applications are received. How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their applications online through the official application portal . Prospective fellows should carefully complete their applications and prepare for the interview process following submission. title: CDEMA Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026: Fully Supported Caribbean Disaster Management Internship for Young Graduates Now Open
summary: The Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) has officially announced applications for its highly anticipated Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026 , offering young graduates across participating Caribbean states a unique opportunity to gain practical experience, build professional skills, and contribute to disaster risk management and resilience-building efforts throughout the region. The internship programme is designed to empower the next generation of disaster management professionals by providing hands-on learning experiences, mentorship opportunities, and exposure to regional disaster management initiatives. Through this programme, CDEMA aims to strengthen youth participation and leadership in Comprehensive Disaster Management (CDM) while creating pathways for future employment and professional growth. Successful candidates will be based at the CDEMA Headquarters in Bridgetown, Barbados, for a period of 12 to 18 months and will receive a monthly stipend along with relocation support for international participants. Application Deadline: July 3, 2026 About the Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) The Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) is the regional inter-governmental agency responsible for coordinating disaster management initiatives among participating Caribbean states. CDEMA works to enhance the capacity of Caribbean countries to effectively manage hazards, reduce disaster risks, and build resilient communities. Through its Comprehensive Disaster Management strategy, the organization promotes sustainable development, preparedness, response, recovery, and resilience-building across the region. Recognizing the critical role of young people in shaping resilient societies, CDEMA has developed the Young Professionals Programme to cultivate future leaders within the disaster risk management sector. What is the Young Professionals Programme (YPP)? The Young Professionals Programme (YPP) is an internship initiative established to provide opportunities for recent graduates from post-secondary institutions while building the skills and competencies necessary for careers in disaster management and resilience planning. The programme seeks to: Develop future disaster management professionals Promote youth leadership in resilience-building efforts Encourage innovation in disaster risk management Support entrepreneurship within the disaster management sector Strengthen regional disaster preparedness and response capacity Build a skilled workforce capable of supporting Caribbean states during emergencies and recovery efforts The programme follows a practical learning-by-doing approach that enables participants to gain real-world experience while contributing to meaningful projects and initiatives. Internship Location and Duration Selected interns will be based at the CDEMA Headquarters located in Bridgetown, Barbados. Key programme details include: Location: Bridgetown, Barbados Internship Duration: 12 to 18 months Organization: Caribbean Disaster Emergency Management Agency (CDEMA) Competition Reference Number: CDEMA-CU/YPP/03 Application Deadline: July 3, 2026 Applicants should be available for the entire internship period. Areas of Professional Development Throughout the programme, interns will gain valuable experience in several critical areas of disaster management and resilience planning. Participants will have opportunities to work on: 1. Applied Disaster Risk Knowledge Interns will strengthen their understanding of disaster risk reduction strategies and hazard management approaches. 2. Preparedness and Response Planning Participants will gain practical experience supporting emergency preparedness and disaster response initiatives. 3. Community Resilience Planning The programme exposes interns to strategies that strengthen community resilience and disaster preparedness at the local level. 4. Recovery Planning and Implementation Interns will learn how governments and organizations coordinate recovery efforts following disasters. 5. Climate Change and Development Integration Participants will gain insights into integrating climate resilience and disaster risk reduction into national and sector planning processes. 6. Project Administration Interns will develop project management and administrative skills essential for careers in disaster management and development sectors. Financial Benefits and Support One of the most attractive aspects of the Young Professionals Programme is the financial and logistical support provided to successful candidates. Benefits include: Monthly Stipend Successful interns will receive a monthly stipend during their placement. Relocation Assistance Candidates selected from outside Barbados will receive: A return airfare ticket A settlement grant to support relocation expenses This support enables participants from across the Caribbean region to take advantage of the opportunity regardless of their location. Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the programme, applicants must meet specific eligibility criteria. Age Requirement Applicants must be: Between 21 and 30 years old Nationality Requirement Applicants must be nationals or citizens of CDEMA Participating States. Academic Qualifications Applicants should possess: A first degree in Earth Science, Social Science, Disaster Management, or a related field Preferred Experience The following will be considered an advantage: Two years of subject-matter experience Experience within disaster management organizations Experience within civil defense agencies Experience with affiliated organizations working in resilience, emergency response, or disaster risk reduction Required Skills and Competencies CDEMA is seeking highly motivated candidates who demonstrate strong technical and interpersonal abilities. Desired skills include: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite Knowledge of project management principles Strong written and verbal communication skills in English Research and analytical skills Ability to understand technical documentation Strong stakeholder engagement abilities Flexibility and adaptability Problem-solving capabilities Data acquisition and analysis skills Attention to detail Critical thinking abilities Teamwork and collaboration skills Initiative and willingness to learn Appreciation for workplace diversity Strong interpersonal skills These competencies will help participants succeed in a dynamic regional working environment. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare and submit the following documents: A Statement of Interest (maximum two pages) Two reference letters Copies of certificates and academic transcripts The Statement of Interest should clearly outline: Academic qualifications Relevant subject knowledge Professional experience Career aspirations Interest in disaster management and resilience-building Applications should be emailed to: hr@cdema.org Applicants must quote the competition reference number: CDEMA-CU/YPP/03 in the subject line of the email. Why You Should Apply The CDEMA Young Professionals Programme is an exceptional opportunity for young graduates interested in disaster management, climate resilience, emergency response, community development, and sustainable development. Participants will gain valuable regional experience, expand their professional networks, develop highly sought-after technical skills, and contribute to building safer and more resilient Caribbean communities. For aspiring professionals seeking a meaningful and impactful career path, the YPP offers an outstanding platform to launch a successful future in disaster risk management and resilience leadership. title: PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship 2026: A Unique Human Rights Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Committed to Global Justice
summary: Are you passionate about human rights, social justice, advocacy, grassroots activism, and international solidarity? The PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity for emerging leaders to deepen their understanding of human rights protection while supporting the work of frontline human rights defenders around the world. The Solidarity Fellowship is a leadership and advocacy initiative organized by Peace Brigades International-USA (PBI-USA), an organization dedicated to protecting human rights defenders and promoting nonviolent approaches to conflict transformation. Through this fellowship, participants gain hands-on experience in advocacy, communications, movement-building, and international solidarity while contributing to the protection of activists facing threats and violence in some of the world s most challenging environments. The fellowship is designed for individuals who are committed to advancing justice, amplifying marginalized voices, and supporting communities working to defend human rights, environmental justice, land rights, indigenous rights, and democratic freedoms. About Peace Brigades International-USA (PBI-USA) Peace Brigades International (PBI) is an internationally recognized non-governmental organization founded in 1981 with a mission to protect human rights and promote the nonviolent transformation of conflicts. For more than four decades, PBI has worked alongside threatened human rights defenders, providing protective accompaniment and international support that helps create safer environments for activists and civil society organizations. PBI s work spans multiple countries where human rights defenders face risks due to their efforts to promote justice, accountability, environmental protection, indigenous rights, gender equality, and peacebuilding. The organization s presence and international networks help deter violence and create space for local activists to continue their important work. The Solidarity Fellowship reflects PBI-USA s commitment to building a new generation of human rights advocates who understand the importance of international solidarity and grassroots action. What is the PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship? The Solidarity Fellowship is a professional development and leadership opportunity that allows fellows to engage directly with human rights advocacy efforts while learning about international human rights protection mechanisms. The fellowship provides participants with practical experience and exposure to: Human rights advocacy International solidarity campaigns Communications and storytelling Public education and awareness initiatives Community organizing Policy engagement Nonviolent conflict transformation Movement-building strategies Human rights accompaniment models Fellows become part of a network of activists, advocates, and organizations dedicated to advancing human rights and supporting communities at risk. Why the Fellowship Matters Across the world, human rights defenders often face intimidation, criminalization, threats, surveillance, and violence because of their work. Many defenders advocate for: Indigenous rights Women s rights Environmental protection Land rights Community development Freedom of expression Access to justice Democratic participation PBI s work focuses on creating safer environments for these defenders by mobilizing international attention and solidarity. Through the fellowship, participants gain a deeper understanding of the challenges faced by activists globally and learn how international networks can contribute to their protection. Fellowship Learning Areas Participants in the Solidarity Fellowship can expect to strengthen their knowledge and skills in several key areas. 1. Human Rights Advocacy Fellows learn how advocacy campaigns are developed and implemented to support threatened communities and human rights defenders. 2. Strategic Communications The programme helps participants understand how storytelling, media engagement, and public education can amplify important human rights issues. 3. International Solidarity Fellows gain insight into how global networks collaborate to support local movements and protect activists facing risks. 4. Nonviolent Peacebuilding Participants explore approaches to conflict transformation that prioritize dialogue, protection, and community-led solutions. 5. Leadership Development The fellowship helps emerging leaders strengthen their communication, collaboration, and organizational skills. 6. Global Human Rights Issues Participants deepen their understanding of international human rights frameworks and contemporary human rights challenges affecting communities worldwide. Benefits of the PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship The fellowship offers numerous professional and personal development benefits. Professional Experience Participants gain meaningful experience within an internationally respected human rights organization. Mentorship and Networking Fellows have opportunities to engage with experienced advocates, human rights defenders, and professionals working across the human rights sector. Leadership Development The programme helps participants build leadership skills that can support future careers in advocacy, nonprofit management, public policy, law, international development, and social justice. Human Rights Knowledge Participants develop a stronger understanding of international human rights systems and grassroots organizing strategies. Career Advancement The experience gained through the fellowship can strengthen future applications for graduate studies, advocacy roles, nonprofit positions, and international development opportunities. Who Should Apply? The fellowship is ideal for: Emerging human rights advocates Students and recent graduates Social justice organizers Community leaders Aspiring nonprofit professionals Individuals interested in international affairs Peacebuilding practitioners Human rights researchers Advocacy and communications professionals Applicants who are passionate about justice, human rights, and global solidarity are particularly encouraged to apply. How to Apply Interested applicants should visit the official PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship webpage to review current opportunities, eligibility requirements, application instructions, and programme details. Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong application materials that clearly demonstrate their commitment to human rights, advocacy, social justice, and community engagement. Why You Should Consider This Fellowship The PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship is more than a professional opportunity. It is an opportunity to contribute to a global movement that protects human rights defenders and supports communities working for justice and peace. For individuals seeking practical advocacy experience, leadership development, and a deeper understanding of international human rights work, the fellowship offers a powerful platform to learn, grow, and make a meaningful impact. The post PBI-USA Solidarity Fellowship 2026: A Unique Human Rights Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Committed to Global Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mental Health America Youth Policy Accelerator 2026–2027: Earn a $3,000 Stipend While Shaping Youth Mental Health Policy in the United States
summary: Applications Open for Mental Health America s Prestigious Youth Policy Accelerator (YPA) 2026–2027 Young leaders passionate about mental health advocacy, youth empowerment, public policy, peer support, and systems change now have an exciting opportunity to influence mental health policy at the national level. Mental Health America (MHA) has officially opened applications for its 2026–2027 Youth Policy Accelerator (YPA), a highly competitive youth leadership program designed to place young people s voices and priorities at the center of mental health policy discussions. The program will select five exceptional young adults from across the United States and provide them with training, mentorship, policy development experience, networking opportunities, and a $3,000 stipend while working on advancing youth peer support policy. Participants will gain hands-on experience collaborating with experts, advocates, policymakers, and youth leaders while helping shape the future of mental health services for young people nationwide. What is the Mental Health America Youth Policy Accelerator? The Youth Policy Accelerator is a national youth leadership initiative developed by Mental Health America to ensure that young people have meaningful opportunities to influence mental health policies that directly affect their lives and communities. The 2026–2027 cohort will focus specifically on: State Youth Peer Support Policy Youth peer support is increasingly recognized as a powerful tool for improving mental health outcomes among young people. The YPA seeks to strengthen and expand access to peer support programs by equipping participants with the knowledge, skills, and relationships needed to advocate for effective policy change. Participants will help develop youth-centered policy recommendations that can be used by advocates, organizations, and decision-makers across the United States. Program Structure The Youth Policy Accelerator consists of two major phases. Part I: Policy Development and Research July 2026 – September 2026 During the first phase, participants will work together to develop model state legislation and policy recommendations related to youth peer support. Activities include: Learning about youth peer support policy Conducting policy research Interviewing experts and stakeholders Participating in leadership development activities Co-authoring youth-driven policy recommendations Building knowledge of legislative processes Time commitment: Approximately 2–4 hours per week Part II: Advocacy and Policy Action October 2026 – March 2027 The second phase focuses on turning ideas into action. Participants will: Publish policy recommendations Collaborate with the National Youth Peer Support Coalition Educate policymakers and advocates Participate in national advocacy efforts Support implementation strategies Advance youth peer support policy nationwide The phase includes: Twice-monthly virtual check-ins Ongoing mentorship Policy engagement opportunities Public speaking experiences Attend and Speak at a National Conference One of the most exciting components of the program is the opportunity to participate in Mental Health America s major national events. Participants will attend and speak at: Mental Health America Policy Institute and Annual Conference Location: Washington, D.C. Dates: October 6–9, 2026 This event brings together: Policymakers Researchers Mental health professionals Advocates Youth leaders Community organizations Participants will share their work, engage with national leaders, and contribute to important policy conversations. Financial Support $3,000 Stipend Each selected Youth Policy Accelerator member receives: A $3,000 participation stipend This compensation recognizes the value of participants time, expertise, and contributions. Conference Scholarship Participants will also receive full support to attend the Washington, D.C. conference. Covered expenses include: Travel Lodging Conference participation Why Apply? The Youth Policy Accelerator offers far more than a leadership title. Participants gain practical skills, professional experience, and national-level exposure. 1. Build Real Policy Experience Participants learn: Policy analysis Legislative development Policy advocacy Research methods Coalition building 2. Develop Leadership Skills The program strengthens: Public speaking Strategic communication Facilitation Teamwork Advocacy leadership 3. Influence Mental Health Policy Participants directly contribute to policy recommendations and advocacy strategies that may influence state-level systems. 4. Expand Your Professional Network Members connect with: Mental health experts Policymakers Researchers Advocates National youth leaders 5. Join a Lifelong Community Participants become part of Mental Health America s youth leadership network, which includes more than 150 alumni. This network provides: Continued training Leadership opportunities Mentorship Professional development Who Should Apply? Mental Health America encourages applications from young adults with diverse experiences and backgrounds. The organization is particularly interested in applicants who are passionate about improving mental health systems and expanding access to youth peer support. Previous participants have backgrounds in: Research Public policy Mental health advocacy College programs Peer support services Community organizing Youth leadership initiatives The program values lived experience and diverse perspectives. Application Requirements Applicants must address the following topics: Why Youth Peer Support Matters Explain your interest in peer support and why it is important. Experience with Peer Support Programs Examples include: Peer support initiatives Mental health education programs Online support communities Crisis text lines Certified peer specialist roles Experience in Mental Health Advocacy or Policy Share examples of policy, advocacy, leadership, or community engagement work. What You Hope to Gain Describe how the program aligns with your goals. How You Will Contribute Explain how you will support and enrich the experience of fellow participants. Submission Formats Applicants may submit: Essays Video responses Bulleted responses Written submissions: 250–500 words Video submissions: 3–5 minutes Applicants may also provide: Academic papers Articles Websites Blogs Social media projects Advocacy portfolios Research work Important Dates Application Deadline: July 17, 2026 Selection Notifications: July 23, 2026 Program Duration: July 2026 – March 2027 Conference Dates: October 6–9, 2026 Contact Information For questions about the application process or assistance completing the application: Jackie Menjivar Manager of Peer and Youth Advocacy Mental Health America Email: jmenjivar@mhanational.org Click HERE to learn more and apply! Conclusion The Mental Health America Youth Policy Accelerator 2026–2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for young advocates to gain practical policy experience, develop leadership skills, influence mental health systems, and join a national movement advancing youth peer support. With a $3,000 stipend, national conference participation, mentorship, and direct engagement with policymakers and experts, the program provides an ideal platform for emerging leaders who want to create meaningful change in youth mental health policy. For young adults committed to mental health advocacy and systems transformation, this opportunity represents a powerful pathway to leadership, impact, and long-term professional growth. Visit Opportunities for Youth (OFY) to discover more global Fellowship , Health , and Accelerator opportunities. title: Creatives4Peace Youth Online Innovation Challenge by United Nations Development Programme (UNDP): Empowering Young African Creatives to Drive Peace, Dialogue, and Social Cohesion Through Art
summary: The Creatives4Peace Youth Online Innovation Challenge , organized by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) , is a groundbreaking initiative designed to empower young African creatives who are using art, culture, and digital expression as tools for peacebuilding, dialogue, and social cohesion. This opportunity recognizes that creativity is not just a form of expression, but also a powerful force for transforming societies, bridging divides, and fostering unity across communities. This challenge invites young innovators from across Africa to submit their creative projects in digital form. The goal is to highlight how artistic expression can be used to reduce conflict, encourage tolerance, and promote inclusive development in societies that are often divided by social, political, or cultural differences. Selected participants will gain international visibility, professional exposure, and the opportunity to present their work at the Culture and Creative Industries Forum in Cameroon , where they will engage with industry leaders, investors, and fellow creatives from across Africa and the diaspora. Overview of the Creatives4Peace Innovation Challenge The Creatives4Peace initiative focuses on harnessing the power of creative industries to build peaceful and inclusive societies. It encourages submissions that demonstrate how art and creativity can influence positive social change. Applicants are encouraged to submit work that can be shared digitally and that reflects strong themes of peacebuilding, inclusion, tolerance, and cultural unity. Eligible Creative Fields Participants may submit projects in any of the following creative and cultural industries: 1. Performing Arts Projects may include: Music compositions and performances Short films and documentaries Dance productions Theatre and stage performances Spoken word poetry and storytelling Performance-based artistic works 2. Gaming and Interactive Media This includes: Video games and mobile games Board games and physical games Educational simulations and puzzles Gamified learning tools Interactive storytelling platforms 3. Digital Art and Multimedia Projects may include: Graphic design and illustration Animation and motion graphics Photography and videography Digital storytelling projects Multimedia installations and interactive visuals 4. Fashion and Wearable Design This category includes: Clothing and textile design Sustainable fashion and upcycling Costume design Accessories and wearable art Fashion-based creative enterprises 5. Crafts and Artisan Work Eligible works include: Handmade crafts and artisan products Woodwork and ceramics Jewelry design and beadwork Textile weaving and traditional crafts Painting and visual craft production Benefits for Selected Participants Selected creatives will receive significant opportunities, including: Showcase of their work at the Culture and Creative Industries Forum in Cameroon Fully funded travel and daily subsistence allowance (DSA) covered by UNDP Opportunity to pitch creative projects to private sector partners for investment or collaboration International exposure and visibility across creative industry platforms Networking opportunities with young creatives from Africa and the diaspora Access to future support, partnerships, and professional growth opportunities Additionally, three outstanding participants will be selected for special recognition and invited to present their work at the forum in Cameroon. Application Process Interested applicants must submit their creative work through the official application form provided by UNDP. Submissions must be digital and clearly demonstrate how the creative work contributes to peacebuilding, social cohesion, and dialogue. Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure their submissions clearly reflect originality, creativity, and social impact. APPLY HERE Deadline The application deadline is September (exact date not specified, likely aligned with the September 2026 Culture and Creative Industries Forum in Cameroon) . Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible due to competitive selection. For more information about this opportunity, visit here. Discover more Global Competition Opportunities on OFY here .
